name: gcloud_storage
description: Upload, download, list, and manage files in Google Cloud Storage buckets

action.description: The Cloud Storage action to perform: list_buckets, create_bucket, list_objects, upload_object, download_object, delete_object, get_object_metadata

project_id.description: GCP project ID. If omitted, uses the default gcloud project.

bucket.description: GCS bucket name (without gs:// prefix). Must be lowercase, 3-63 chars, alphanumeric + hyphens.

object_path.description: Object path within the bucket (e.g. 'data/file.csv'). No '..' or leading '/' allowed.

content.description: Text content to upload (for upload_object action with inline content).

local_path.description: Local file path for upload/download operations. Restricted to workspace and temp directories. Requires feature flag.

prefix.description: Object name prefix for filtering list_objects results (e.g. 'data/').

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return for list operations (default: 100, max: 500).

instructions:
Use this tool to interact with Google Cloud Storage. The user authenticates via their own gcloud CLI login.

**Available actions:**
- `list_buckets` — List all buckets in the project
- `create_bucket` — Create a new bucket (requires bucket name, optional location). Requires allow_create_bucket flag.
- `list_objects` — List objects in a bucket (optional prefix filter, max 500 results)
- `upload_object` — Upload inline text content to a bucket. Local file uploads require allow_local_file_upload flag.
- `download_object` — Download an object as text content. Saving to local file requires allow_local_file_download flag.
- `delete_object` — Delete an object from a bucket. Requires allow_delete_objects flag.
- `get_object_metadata` — Get metadata (size, type, updated) for an object

**Security notes:**
- Prefer read-only operations (list, get_object_metadata, download as text). Confirm with user before writes/deletes.
- Bucket names are validated per GCS naming rules (lowercase, 3-63 chars).
- Object paths are validated — no '..' traversal or absolute paths.
- Local file access is sandboxed to workspace and temp directories.
- Upload/download sizes are capped at 50 MB. Use gsutil for larger files.
